name: APRC-CM multimodal protocol moved biological-age markers
summary: A single-arm healthy-aging pilot reported PhenoAge and DNA-methylation age reductions after a multimodal protocol that included autologous pro-regenerative conditioned media.
statement: In a single-arm open-label pilot of 16 healthy adults aged 46 to 72 years, a 17-week multimodal protocol including lifestyle optimization, supplements, and two APRC-CM infusions was associated with reported reductions in PhenoAge and DNA-methylation age, with no adverse events reported.
population_or_model: Healthy adults aged 46 to 72 years; 14 completers in the published analysis
quantitative_note: The accepted article reports PhenoAge -2.0 years and epigenetic age -2.7 years overall, with a larger-response subgroup showing mean epigenetic-age reduction of 5.1 years versus 0.2 years in a smaller/no-response subgroup.
caveats: The trial was single-arm and open-label, with no sham, placebo, or standard-care control.
The intervention combined lifestyle optimization, supplements, and conditioned media, so the conditioned-media or extracellular-vesicle contribution cannot be isolated.
Biological-age marker movement over 17 weeks does not establish durable functional, disease, mortality, healthspan, or LEV-relevant benefit.
The article was an accepted Frontiers version during this review, with final formatted publication still pending.
